title: State-Space Description of a System
summary: Example 1.20 demonstrates how state equations can be derived by inspection for an electrical network with capacitor charge q1 and inductor flux q2 as state variables. The example derives two simultaneous first-order differential equations forming the state equations, showing that once solved, all circuit outputs can be determined via output equations.

figures.caption: Choosing suitable initial conditions in a network.
figures.description: A circuit diagram shows a source labeled x(t) feeding a top rail through a 1 Ω resistor, with branch currents i1(t), iC(t), i2(t), and i3(t) indicated by arrows and element voltages v1(t), v2(t), v3(t), and vL(t) marked with polarity signs. The network includes a 1 F capacitor to the bottom reference node, a 2 Ω vertical resistor, a 2 H inductor in the top path, and a 5 Ω vertical resistor at the right; capacitor charge q1(t) and inductor-related state q2(t) are labeled near the energy-storage elements. Students should notice the common bottom node, the series and shunt relationships among elements, and that the figure highlights suitable initial conditions associated with the capacitor and inductor states.
